And that they're allowed to charge me because it's in their T&Cs. I don't agree, and I reckon the website should be able to handle a family of four with a dog.

My first experience staying at Bendigo  Park Lane Holiday Park with two poodles. The cabins was lovely, clean. The bed was like sleeping on a cloud after a very long drive.  The staff were  terrific, even showing me to my cabin and turning on the aircon to warm up my cabin.
Felt very comfortable and safe there. Was on my own. Clean too.

One of the worst arrival experiences that I have had.  The directions to the site were appalling. The map particularly lacking in detail. My wife specifically asked questions to provide orientation to the directions being given. Once we tried to find the site all became obvious.

When we did find the site it was difficult to find the boundaries. But we did find some sort of crooked white lines amongst the patchy half dead grass and lumps of long ago used concrete.

We have stayed at Big 4 Tasman in Bendigo and we have always had a great experience (although we look at Discovery Parks as being a better option, always).

This park has no meaningful proximity to Bendigo. It has nothing appealing within a reasonable walking distance.

Best advice I can give is to look at other options.
The most amazing play centre, my kid are already hassling to me to go back there just for the play centre!
Close to Bendigo central. Has a play centre inside the big4
Lovely caravan park, easy access from main road, great service from owners, clean facilities, lots of kid activities
Love this place..... 2nd time staying here for the easter weekend.... staff are great and friendly, cabins are cosy and clean... can't rate it highly enough!

My family had a great stay.

Kids had a ball would definitely recommend coming we will be coming back.

It looks like this park is mainly concentrating on cabins and probably do that well. For caravans not so much. I was handed a lot of paperwork, told nothing and discovered they charge for dogs. $118 for two nights of dust

We stayed recently in powered ensuite site with manicured gardens and a lux ensuite that had thick towels, etc.  The larger size of the shower with the rain shower head was superb. The only thing I would suggest is to provide a hair dryer.  Not everyone takes those necessities with them.  The site itself was shaded pretty well with a tree out the front.
The pool area was great.  Shade sails were erected over the toddler pool and spa only.  Not over the main pool, so when the sun was out, they were hot under foot. Also, no safety rail to hold onto made it unsafe for me to use, so I did just use the spa.  For me, the main pool definitely needs a shade sail and safety rail to make it safer for people, particularly with injuries (I have a lower back injury)
The communal kitchen and games room were great, and if you have kids, there is so much for them to do too
I would highly recommend this park to my family and friends, and I definitely would stay here again 💗

I've been meaning to write a review of this park since we stayed with our family. It has been one of my favourite parks that we've stayed at. We stayed during the winter holidays and the children had great fun in the on site play centre. When they closed for the evening they had a large, comfortable and cosy games/ recreation room where we could watch family DVDs that were provided or have a game of pool. It had the kitchen attached so we coul make popcorn and hot chocolate.  The other park residents were friendly and it was a very family friendly park. Even though the weather didn't permit the pool and spa looked very inviting. The location was perfect and not far from town.
The toilets, showers and laundry where all clean and tidy.
Last but not least the park staff were amazing. On our first day it rained heavily and while we were out they laid out a grass mat besides our caravan. We hadn't asked for it but it was a thoughtful and appreciated gesture. We hope to stay here again next time we visit Bendigo.
If you want to camp in a dust bowl this is the place for you. Saw no maintenance being done at all. Caravan sites quite difficult because of uneven & broken edging.  Never to return.
Relaxing with 5 star ensuites for their powered van sites. Pools, spa, camp kitchen, awesome playground for the kids. Super relaxing.
Good for kids soft play

Paid in full for a powered sites 8 months in advance of stay for The Red Hot Suumer Tour. The dates of concert changed and we were charged a moving date fee. That's fine paid that. We received a phone call a month later to say that another concert Move in the Groove was on the same weekend therefore the site has been repriced at over an extra $100 per night per site. Is that legal after we agreed to the original price and fully paid.
Beautiful rooms, modern and spacious
New cabins and ensuite sites. A little bit away from town.
Rang up late, they found a cabin and upgraded us. Close to everything cheers for stay

Upgrades in this park are fantastic. We had a fully concreted site with a modern spacious ensuite. Landscaped  gardens compliment the sites.  It was a long weekend with reasonable rates considering the ensuite was fully serviced with towels and toiletries.   A little far out from the centre of Bendigo but still a great location.
Fun place for families, however, some areas of the park need maintenance.
Plenty to do for kids. Beautiful and clean enjoyed our visit

Absolutely appalling. What we thought was going to be the ideal holiday was far from ideal. I’m not sure how this park qualifies as a BIG 4. We stayed there for about a week, our cabin was in the back corner which was nice and reserved. Upon arrival the cabin was nice and cool, the AC had been turned on. The cabin was cool however the bedrooms felt like a sauna. The dust, spider webs, stains and dirt stood out in majority of the cabin.The pool was quite small and the go karts were not as advertised. The customer service was dreadful!! The kids liked the indoor playcenter but apart from that, that was really the main thing there at Big 4 Bendigo Park Lane. With that lack of customer care I certainly will not be returning.

Booked in to camp for 4 days. Our site was rocky and full of clay. I chose the highest part. It rained and the tent stayed dry but walking mud in from outside. Plus stones poking thru tent floor. The front of the site had a dip. The site was on quite a large slope. Changed to a cabin. The cabin was a budget one but very nice.
Took the grandchildren to Parkies. Wasn't too crowded.  Children enjoyed the play equipment.

This park is worker friendly,  and gave my company a great corporate rate, and a free upgrade for part of my stay.
Really great staff,  and impeccable attention to detail and cleanliness for a caravan park. I've stayed in the budget through to what my staff called "The Emperors Palace" - a 9 person family suite- comes with the hugest TV, x 2 bathrooms,  beautiful kitchen and the perfect BBQ area of your own.

This is an exemplary caravan park,  by far the best of so many I've stayed in as a travelling worker. Great parking,  well laid out park,  quiet (can't hear the main road) and something so important - the best, comfy beds with really good linen.

Pricing is very fair - you won't find better in Bendigo 😉

Bitterly disappointed, the park was nowhere near what we expected for a BIG4. The photos on website are outdated and the sites are not well looked after, our site desperately needed a mow and was not up to standard at all to any of the other BIG4 parks we have been to. Our site was in the back corner, tucked away from everything which was nice, until the sewers flooded and we were camping and stepping in sewer water. My partner even had to sleep in his swag in it. After that we had had enough and decided to pack up early and check out the next morning and because there had been so much rain and the sewers flooded our caravan got bogged and we could not get it out. Luckily some other campers came to our aid (much more than any of the staff) and winched out our caravan. During this my daughter went up to reception to ask if there was anything they could do to help, where they degraded her and cut her off. When maintenance finally arrived 25 minutes later, he stood there and laughed and said ‘well it’s not my fault’ and offered no help when my husband explained to him that the reason our caravan got bogged was because they had dug a trench for a power line and not backfilled it or compacted it down, so our van simply sunk into the trench with all the rain that softened the soil. The other campers were lovely and it ended up taking a a triton winching a ranger to get it out. All in all we will NOT be returning and are extremely disappointed in the customer service and the state of the park.

We stayed here  in cabin 10  the orange
3bdr  cabin for 10 days.  Staff were very friendly and helpful I couldn't fault them.

Just an in depth review for anyone

As for the cabin itself it was a bit of a let down . For a 9 person cabin the fridge was tiny to put food in for 9 people .  On arrival the toilet smelt like really bad b.o , could possibly be the toilet seat itself ,  there was very little pantry space to put things but we rearranged where the bowls and plates were to accommodate the lack of pantry space. But we put everything Bak how we found things. Knifes were blunt  couldn't find a sharpener.
Cooling wise the lounge area is great but needs something further for the back 2 rooms on hot days it's a sauna well not that hot but U get the jist.
Oven and grill worked perfectly and easy to use. Ohhhh and one last thing about the cabin whoever cleans it can U plz move the couches out and clean there. Looks dusty as and looks like it hasn't been done for months.

The deck was a bit small for a cabin for 9. If you are a bigger person U would almost have to ask them to get up and push the chair in to get through.

We have stayed at many many big 4s , this pool was small I'll leave it at that.

The actual park has lots of entertainment and activities for the kids to run wild. A indoor play centre for the kids.

In reality I would come back but would try another Cabin

As a family of 4 adults with a furbaby, we thought that finding accommodation to suit our needs at the spare of the moment, right on new years day would be impossible.
We were recommended this caravan park and upon looking online, booking a cabin was so easy and straight forward.
Upon arrival we were met by the sweetest and friendliest young reception staff, who advised us that we were kindly ( for know reason other than luck) upgraded from budget cabin that we booked to family cabin.
The cabin was purple number 6 and oh my gosh ..
The entire park is gorgeous,  clean, friendly and so well designed, cared for.
The amenities were spotless and so kid friendly.
The price was very reasonable
Our adult bed was super comfy and upon entering our cabin , the floors had been freshly mopped and aircon on for our comfort.
Such a lovely size cabin too
We can not wait to return for longer at Easter

Massive thankyou to owners and staff for such a wonderful caravan, cabin park in beautiful bendigo

We had a powered ensuite site.  Ensuite was clean, site neat and tidy.  Had trouble getting van in due to dip,  park had ramps to lift vehicle, no trouble at all.  Great games room, indoor play room, jumping pillow. Pet friendly, accommodation for any type of traveller.  Has pool and spa.  Found pool to be full of bugs, but kids swam anyway.  9 minute drive from city centre.
Is a nice, friendly, family park.  Would stay again

Incredibly  dissapointed at their lack of customer  service when doing a group booking 12 months early to secure spots next to family and friends  to find out on arrival  that they have split us from our group. No attempt  to fix, resolve or compensate for the inconvenience of having to set up camp twice in 24hours. Will definitely  not be visiting again.
Awesome place for kids and parents.

Facilities were great, ensuite sites were beautiful and clean, however woke up on the last day to no hot water, obviously gas had ran out. I understand these things happen however we don’t pay a good price to not be able to have a hot shower before our trip home.
Also not sure if there are any other ensuite sites within the park but if not, maybe just go a powered site as far away from the neighbours as possible unless you want an air compressor going off at 2am & 4am followed by a rooster starting at 4am each morning.

A bit overpriced for an unpowered site for 1 night, $56. The bathrooms were nice, and the pool and spa were relaxing. However, the unpowered sites are at the front, next to the highway and water pump, so it was a very noisy stay.
If you're going to throw the unpowered sites out on the front lines, at least lower the price 😘
One thing I was not happy with people in the cabins over from us 2am sitting outside talking stopped about 2.30 am not happy apart from that we had a good time.
Only been here to take the kids to the playroom and had my daughter party here. So much better than jumps. Great place. Probably more suited for 3yo+

Friendly caravan park close to Bendigo centre (5 min by car). Has nearby Coles plus petrol station close. Park itself is well laid out with various types of accommodation from camping to villas. There is a pool and spa plus play centre and various areas for kids to play. Dog friendly also. Can get busy so best to pre book. Great value, we had budget onsuit site and were very happy with location

Big thanks for Sonja  and Ian and staff for accommodating us in the sad time the service is excellent the rooms where clean very very accommodating I would recommend this park I give them a 10/10 amazing hope to be back under different circumstances sue fenn and families
Went there for a joint birthday party. Great venue. The cabins looked clean and still plenty of space for caravans and tents. Really enjoyed it

An excellent park for familles.The cabins are comfortable. Large & spotless . The customer service was outstanding . There was a glitch with our booking Annmarie & katie went above and beyond to rectify it. Thank you girls & the park-lane team .

We’ll definitely be back .

An excellent set up for families. The cabins were clean and beds very comfortable. The service was outstanding. There was a glitch with our booking and the team went above and beyond to rectify it. The team on-site quite obviously cared that they were offering the best experience to their guests. We will definitely go back.

A lovely spot right on the rail trail. Close drive to town. Plenty of facilities and activities for all ages, and super friendly staff. Kids especially will love this place with a jumping mat, indoor play centre, small train rides around the caravan park etc.
Very comfortable cabins well maintained clean undercover parking at our unit.  Quiet park pool play equipment  family friendly

Definitely recommend, brightly coloured and well maintained caravan park with plenty of accommodation options, fantastic facilities with massive indoor play center, playground, heated spa, well equipped camp kitchen, bbq area and games room. Clean and modern facilities and only just a short drive from center of town.
Awesome place to stay, would happily recommend to family and friends.

We had a great 3 night here. Kids had plenty to do, to keep them busy and happy! Pool, spa, pedal carts, jumping pillow, indoor play centre, basketball ring, outdoor play area, pool table, table tennis, outdoor play ground etc

We stayed in an unpowered en-suite site for 1 night in our roof top tent. This was just a stop over for us on our way back to NSW. En-suite is very modern and looks like it has just been updated. Nice hot shower and clean bathroom. Fresh towels supplied which was a nice surprise. The park is right next to the highway which makes it a little loud at time. Overall a fine stop over for one night.
great place for a short visit.  no internet though which was really annoying.  only if u sat outside could u connect

Tegan really saved us with a very last minute booking after our free camp disaster. We stayed in a pod and it was beautiful. Everything was clean and so cutely decorated. From one holiday park worker to another, you guys are amazing. 100% sure we will be back next time we are in Bendigo

This park is amazing for kids
As older couple we saw and heard no kids they have amazing fun park
Road noise yes but they seem to be addressing it with fencing and bushes and people need to understand this takes time
It did affect us as we were close but park was obviously addressing it
Our shower ensuite is amazing
Construction happening for more
I find price okay excellent value if you have kids
We have camper not cabin
Close to a great social club venue for meals and entertainment
A good place to stay just out of the city
Stayed at a new ensuite site which was good. Park does the job but is on a major road which is noisy. Neighbours rooster starting up at 4am isn't ideal.

Attended a birthday party at Parkys. The kids, 3 year old party, had a great time. The party room was well set up. Cold drinks, tea and coffee available.
The high light was the train ride around the park, I think the kids enjoyed it as well.

I’m surprised that this park meets Big4 standards. The playgrounds are all dated, the basketball court if you can call, a 5x5m piece of concrete with a homemade ring a basketball court, had no lines left on it and constantly chasing the ball off the road. Next was the amount of trip hazards with all the uneven ground and concrete edging around. The amount of mud made the whole experience seem depressing. Not to mention that it was 5km from Bendigo town centre. If looking for accomodation I would suggest somewhere kept in better condition. The indoor play centre is filthy and smells really bad. We had to leave because it looked and smelt like it had never been cleaned. There was so much dust over the top of it that it. If your going to sell accomodation I think that this park needs to hold a better standard.

Our first time at Park Lane and will definitely come again. The grounds are well kept, the kids were never bored and a 5 min drive to Bendigo CBD. The staff are amazing and the service is warm and genuine, which is very hard to find these day. Kids highlight was the tractor train ride! Will be back for sure!

More of a buyer beware warning and my fault for not checking the price. We paid $143.10 for our lovely little swamp even the fake grass was half submerged. When I enquired about the cost I was informed it was school holidays so if you intend bringing the kids bring plenty of spare clothes and gumboots. They have plenty of mud and water to keep them busy😉😉
Love it here fun for all ages and very relaxed .

Don’t Even Bother Stopping here!!

The service was terrible
The bathrooms don’t lock
The doors don’t shut properly
The “Unpowered Sites “ where a mud pit which where not numbered but was rudely told they are in fact which you will see in my photo there are no clear markings was told why didn’t you park some where else with us meant to know where no one else was staying.

The Dog Run would make good for a turtle run far from a dog run.

I will never recommend or be back to this place

We where meant to stay for 2 nights but left after zero sleep on the first.

Absolutely appalling. DO NOT STAY.

If you are after unpowered camping I highly recommend not stopping in here.

The owner of the park disrespectfully said and I quote “why do you want an unpowered site” as she rolled her eyes at my friends partner.

You will be situated in a muddy site parallel to the highway. ‘Allocated’ camping is a big stretch as they have white spray painted faded numbers on a white fence. Go figure.

Lastly. They have a very small “dog run” suitable for a small to miniature dog.

Do your research. Do not stay here.
Great friendly bunch of girls and the room was fantastic

Look, overall the staff and the facilities here are great. There are plenty of things to do with the kids and only 7 minutes out of town..

The downside of this place is that the indoor playground is open to the public.  If you stay there over a weekend... good luck trying to even get in there with your kids. They hold up to 8 parties every day on the weekend, making it absolutely useless for kids who stay there to use,  as it's too full with other kids, and there is absolutely nowhere for parents who are paying to stay at the park, to sit and watch their kids...
We went for a weekend stay with my whole family, and on Saturday, our kids couldn't use the place at all as it was full of party kids, and there was nowhere for us to sit!
The increased traffic flow of people coming to the park to go to the parties is a bit concerning, and it made us not want to have our kids ride around the park.

Also, the games room in the camp kitchen area was a little run down and was a bit miserable looking.  The games tables were broken like the pool table had broken pockets, the air hockey table was lop sided and the scoring system didn't work.

Our cabin had a few cleaning and maintenance issues. We had no gas in our cabin on arrival and the cleanliness of the shower was a bit bad. There was old soap sittin on top of the shower and grime all around the shower door and handle. And most of the dishes were dirty in the cupboards so obviously not checked by the cleaning staff..

The staff were absolutely amazing though, some of the most friendliest staff we've ever met! So that was a bonus.

But given the playcentre is open to the public and you can't sit and relax with your kids, would put me off going to this park again.
